content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 6, 1988


Mary M. Miyaslina
15920 Youngwood
Whittier, CA 90603

Re: Enclosed Copy of Advice
Regarding Slate Mailers
Our File No. A-88-186

Dear Ms. Miyaslina:

The enclosed letter is sent to you because records on file
with the Secretary of State's Office indicate that you are an
officer with, or a participant in, a registered slate mailer
organization. We want to be sure that you are aware of the
Commission's interpretation of the requirements regarding
disclaimers on slate mailers.

Please review the enclosed letter. We believe that it is
self-explanatory. However, if you have questions you may reach
me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

Letter No. A-88-186

Enclosure
Letter No. A-88-186


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

UCLA Law School
405 Hilgard
Los Angeles, California 90024

May 12, 1988

Robert Leigh
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Bob,

This is a request for advice regarding interpretation of
Government Code Section 84305.5. The request is made on behalf
of two of my clients, each of whom expects to publish slate mail
subject to that section. The clients are Barad & Levine, and
Voter Guide 88.

In various circumstances, the requirement of paragraph
(a)(1) that the name and address of the slate mailer organization
be printed, and the requirement of paragraph (a)(2) that certain
information including the name of the slate mailer organization
be printed in a box apply to the same page or surface of a slate
mailing. The question I am presenting is whether, under such
circumstances, the requirements may be satisfied by including the
address with the name where the name appears inside the required
box.

As always, I appreciate your cooperation.

Best wishes,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Attorney

sections.question_synthetic: What are the FPPC's requirements regarding disclaimers on slate mailers that officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations should be aware of?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is providing guidance on disclaimer requirements for slate mailers to officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations, with the enclosed letter containing the specific interpretation and requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a mass mailing of transmittal letters without traditional Q&A sections; the substantive advice letter (A-88-186) is referenced as enclosed but not fully included in the provided text. Multiple recipients identified (13+). Letters follow identical template structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the FPPC's requirements regarding disclaimers on slate mailers that officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations should be aware of?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is providing guidance on disclaimer requirements for slate mailers to officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations, with the enclosed letter containing the specific interpretation and requirements.

embedding.summary: This document consists of multiple identical transmittal letters dated July 6, 1988, sent by FPPC General Counsel Diane M. Griffiths to various slate mailer organization officers and participants. Each letter encloses advice (Letter No. A-88-186) regarding the FPPC's interpretation of disclaimer requirements for slate mailers.
